title: 10 best herbal teas for ringworm

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera, including salicylic acid and saponins, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection spreading.

Softering and protective properties: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects help calm irritated skin, reduce scratching, and create a protective barrier against further fungal infection and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the plant has been used for centuries to treat various skin issues, including fungal infections like ringworm. The tea's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, work to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal. Regular consumption of aloe vera tea may help reduce inflammation and speed up the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin has antifungal properties that help combat the fungal infections responsible for ringworm, thereby reducing its severity and promoting healing.
Saponins: Saponins in Aloe vera have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which aid in eliminating the fungal pathogens that cause ringworm and preventing further infections.

Acemannan: Acemannan exhibits immunomodulatory effects, which help stimulate the body's immune response to fight off the fungal infection, thereby accelerating the healing process and reducing the symptoms of ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are widely used to make teas due to their high concentration of antifungal and antibacterial compounds that help combat ringworm.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is also commonly used for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in healing ringworm-infected skin.
Mucilage from Leaves: The mucilage, a thick, protective substance found in the leaves, is used to create teas that help to soothe and protect the skin from further infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature leaves of aloe vera from your garden or store-bought plant in morning.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with cool water and gently scrub off any dirt or debris.
Cut off the green outer layer of the leaves with a sharp knife to expose the clear gel inside.
Scoop out 2-3 tablespoons of the clear gel from the inside of the aloe vera leaf and blend it with 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Certain herbal teas like tea tree oil-infused tea or tea made from herbs like goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) and myrrh (Commiphora molmol) have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Teas like calendula (Calendula officinalis) and plantain (Plantago major) may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery.

Antimicrobial properties: Teas made from herbs like garlic (Allium sativum), ginger (Zingiber officinale), and chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, teas helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia, such as terpinen-4-ol, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, including Microsporum and Trichophyton species. The antiseptic properties of tea tree tea help to soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments for ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These phenolic compounds help combat ringworm by exhibiting antifungal properties that inhibit the growth and spread of fungal pathogens, such as Trichophyton spp.

Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that contribute to the treatment of ringworm by reducing inflammation, killing fungal cells, and promoting a healthy environment for skin repair.
Linalool: This terpene exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the infection and soothe the affected skin, thereby facilitating the recovery from ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of antibacterial and antifungal compounds, which help combat ringworm infections.
Buds: Buds are utilized for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in soothing and healing ringworm lesions.
Leaves: Leaves are also used due to their high oil content, particularly tea tree oil, which is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties that help treat ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and stems from a reputable supplier.
Boil 250ml of water and let it cool for 5 minutes to avoid scalding.
Steep the dried plant material in the water for 5-7 minutes to release oils.
Strain the liquid using a tea infuser or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving or refrigerating.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Pot marigold tea contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that inhibit fungal growth, effectively combating the fungus that causes ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's saponins and flavonoids promote collagen synthesis, improve wound hydration, and enhance tissue repair, which aids in the healing of skin lesions caused by ringworm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antimicrobial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm. Additionally, calendula's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with the condition. By promoting a healthy balance of skin flora, calendula tea can aid in the treatment and prevention of ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity, which can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): As potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, aiding in the recovery from ringworm.
Carotenoids (Violaxanthin and Antheraxanthin): These pigments have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritation and promote healing in cases of ringworm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of saponins and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected skin.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have been shown to have antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections such as ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after the dew has dried completely for best results.
Clean the flowers thoroughly by gently rinsing them in cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the flowers using a dehydrator set to a low temperature of 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal infusion within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Ginkgo biloba tea contains antifungal compounds such as terpenoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate the healing process and prevent complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which help combat the fungal infection that causes the condition. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity and duration of ringworm. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further infection. This makes Ginkgo biloba a potential natural remedy for ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, which could help combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, including ginkgolides and bilobalide, have been found to possess antifungal properties, which could help inhibit the growth of dermatophytes and reduce symptoms of ringworm.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including vanillic acid and ferulic acid, have been reported to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, which could help control the spread of ringworm and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used to make teas due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used to make teas, particularly for their reported antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Rinse the dried leaves with clean water to remove any impurities or debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves to enjoy the herbal tea.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which exhibit antimicrobial activity against fungi, bacteria, and viruses, including those that cause ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenolic compounds in lavender tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: Lavender tea's antifungal properties are particularly effective against Candida spp., a common cause of fungal infections like ringworm, and can help prevent the spread of the infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, including Trichophyton species. Additionally, the tea's soothing and calming effects may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery. Its natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties also aid in preventing infections and promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal infections that cause ringworm.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of the fungal infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antifungal and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating the fungal infection and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which may help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Used for their calming and antiseptic effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Stems: Used due to their potential antiseptic properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the late morning when the dew has evaporated and the essential oils are at their peak.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm shaded area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the oils.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the dried flowers and retain the liquid.
Serve the lavandula angustifolia tea hot or chilled within 30 minutes of preparation to ensure the best flavor and aroma.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Garlic's antimicrobial compounds, such as allicin and ajoene, can inhibit fungal growth and reduce symptoms of ringworm infection, including itching, redness, and scaling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and sulfur-containing compounds in garlic tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling, itching, and discomfort associated with ringworm infection.

Antibacterial activity: Garlic's antibacterial properties can also contribute to its effectiveness against ringworm, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that may be present on the skin or in the affected area, and preventing secondary infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin and ajoene, exhibit potent antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm. The antifungal properties of garlic help to inhibit the growth and spread of the fungal infection, promoting healing and reducing symptoms. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of garlic help to prevent secondary bacterial infections.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur-containing compound found in garlic, has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm.
S-allylcysteine (SAC): SAC, a stable form of allicin, has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of ringworm by inhibiting fungal growth.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in garlic, has been reported to possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat the fungal infection associated with ringworm.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make teas because they are rich in compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make teas because they contain sulfur compounds that have been shown to inhibit fungal growth.
Buds: Buds are used to make teas because they contain volatile oils that have antiseptic and antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Select 1-2 tablespoons of fresh allium sativum leaves and 1-2 inches of root for every 8 ounces of water.
Rinse the allium sativum leaves and root under cold running water for 2-3 minutes.
Combine the fresh allium sativum leaves and root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser.
Steep the allium sativum mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches your desired strength.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve immediately while still warm.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.

Berbamine: Exhibits potent antiviral and antibacterial activity, which assists in fighting off the causative agent of ringworm (Trichophyton spp.) and other microorganisms that contribute to the infection.

Amino Acids: Goldenseal contains a range of amino acids, including berberine-bound amino acids, which help to stimulate the immune system and promote its natural response to fungal infections like ringworm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, teas helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The berberine content in goldenseal has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, effectively treating ringworm infections. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm. As a natural remedy, goldenseal tea provides a gentle and non-invasive approach to treating ringworm, promoting healthy skin and tissue regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid helps with ringworm by exhibiting antifungal properties, which inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause the infection.
Hydrastine: This alkaloid has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms and prevent the spread of the infection.
Vasophenol: This phenolic compound possesses antifungal and antiseptic properties, which contribute to the treatment of ringworm by inhibiting fungal growth and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis are primarily used due to their high content of berberine, an alkaloid with antifungal properties that help combat ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which aid in soothing and treating skin infections, including ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Hydrastis canadensis contain berberine and other compounds that exhibit antifungal activity, making them effective in treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of the plant in the fall after the first frost to ensure optimal potency has been reached.
Dry the roots by spreading them out in a single layer on a wire rack for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their active properties.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powdered root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup to create a tea.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes before straining and serving to allow the flavors to meld together.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions like prostatitis or enlarged prostate.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in saw palmetto tea may protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which could contribute to its potential benefits for urinary tract health and prostate issues.

Estrogen-balancing: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) may help regulate hormone levels and alleviate symptoms related to benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) or hormonal imbalances.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, including those that cause ringworm. The antifungal properties of Serenoa repens help to reduce the severity and duration of ringworm symptoms. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with ringworm. This makes Serenoa repens a popular natural remedy for ringworm treatment.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Serenoa repens teas may help with ringworm by exhibiting antifungal properties, which can inhibit the growth of fungi and promote a healthy skin environment.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Serenoa repens teas may help with ringworm by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fungal infections.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Serenoa repens teas may help with ringworm by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can inhibit the growth of fungi and other microorganisms that cause skin infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain bioactive compounds that are believed to have antifungal properties, which can help combat ringworm infections.
Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of saponins and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used because they contain similar bioactive compounds to the roots, which can help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried serenoa repens root and leaves from a reputable source.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried plant material.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the plant material.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm.
Antifungal activity: The tea's antifungal properties, particularly due to the presence of beta-sitosterol, can help combat fungal infections such as ringworm by inhibiting their growth and spread.

Antioxidant activity: Stinging nettle tea is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, making it easier for the body to fight off infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, teas helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The tea's active compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of ringworm-causing fungi. Additionally, stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm. The tea's soothing effects can also promote wound healing and reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms, making it a potential natural remedy for this common fungal infection.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: As a powerful flavonoid antioxidant, quercetin helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ringworm infections, promoting healing and tissue repair.
Fucosterol: This steroid saponin has antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms.

Alkaloids (specifically, gramine and dihydrogramine): These compounds possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can help combat the fungal infection causing ringworm, reducing the risk of transmission and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing skin irritations and promoting healing.
Roots: Containing compounds that have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, helping to combat fungal infections that cause ringworm.
Stems: Abundant in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in treating skin conditions like ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves and stems of urtica dioica plant at dawn or early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours to preserve medicinal properties.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to enjoy the potential health benefits of urtica dioica.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, exhibits antifungal activity against Candida albicans, one of the most common fungi associated with ringworm, helping to combat fungal infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin and other polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting healing and tissue repair.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin tissues and hinder the body's natural healing response to ringworm infections, promoting overall skin health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton and Microsporum species. Additionally, curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce itching and irritation associated with ringworm infections, making turmeric tea a promising natural remedy for treating and preventing ringworm. Its efficacy has been observed in various traditional and modern studies.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a polyphenolic compound that exhibits potent antifungal properties, which can help in inhibiting the growth and proliferation of fungi causing ringworm.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of ringworm.
Turmerone: Turmerone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which can help in combating the fungal infection causing ringworm.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making teas due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are also widely used for their curcumin content, which helps in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in ringworm-infected skin.
Leaves: Leaves are occasionally used for making teas as they contain some amount of curcumin, although in lower concentrations compared to rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 inches of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and cut them into small pieces.
Dry the curcuma longa pieces in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to 3 hours.
Grind 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1/2 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it immediately while it is still warm to enjoy the benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The tea's active compounds, such as citral and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida and Aspergillus species, commonly responsible for ringworm outbreaks. Additionally, lemon verbena's soothing and calming effects can reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena tea has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm, inhibiting the spread of the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena tea can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief from symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena tea's antioxidant properties can help to protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, roots help to combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Buds: Containing potent antifungal properties, buds aid in inhibiting the growth of fungal spores that cause ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems from a trusted source, ensuring a minimum of 100g for this recipe.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry thoroughly.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and 500ml of boiling water in a large glass teapot, allowing it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids before adding honey to taste if desired.
Enjoy the warm, soothing verbena officinalis tea within 30 minutes of preparation, taking 3 times daily as needed for ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to inhibit fungal growth, reduce redness and swelling, and promote a healthy immune response. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial properties help to prevent secondary infections and promote wound healing. By incorporating American witch hazel teas into their regimen, individuals can effectively manage ringworm symptoms and support overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: The tannins present in American Witch Hazel tea help to constrict and dry out fungal infections like ringworm, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Antifungal Activity: The plant's polyphenolic compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties against various species of fungi, including those that cause ringworm, helping to prevent the spread of the infection.
Anti-inflammatory Action: Witch Hazel tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing of skin lesions.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that combats fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hamamelis virginiana leaves and stems by cutting them in early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or trays for 1-2 weeks.
Crush dried hamamelis virginiana into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steep 1 teaspoon of crushed plant material in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create the tea infusion.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and drink the cooled tea 2-3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with ringworm because it possesses antifungal properties that can effectively combat the infection. The tea's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm. By consuming yarrow tea regularly, individuals can help speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms, making it a natural and effective remedy for treating this common skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, making it more manageable.
Antimicrobial activity: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties, specifically its ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, can help combat the underlying infection causing ringworm.

Wound-healing promotion: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow tea may also promote wound healing by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and protecting against further damage or infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in soothing skin irritations associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for the tea mixture.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for several hours.
Combine one teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium herb with one cup of boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the solids, then add honey to taste if desired.
Drink the cooled tea three times daily, ideally at morning and evening, for a period of six weeks or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which work to combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The antimicrobial compounds found in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for treating ringworm. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe itching and discomfort associated with ringworm, providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of this fungal infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Ginger's antifungal compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, including gingerols and salicylates, can reduce swelling and redness associated with ringworm lesions, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's high antioxidant content can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections like ringworm.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antifungal compounds, rhyzomes are used to treat fungal infections like ringworm due to their ability to inhibit fungal growth.
Roots: Deep-seated roots contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to combat ringworm and other skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried ginger root powder in two cups of water for five minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl to remove solids.
Combine the strained tea liquid with one teaspoon of lemon juice and stir well to enhance absorption and flavor.
Let the tea solution cool down to room temperature for at least thirty minutes before storing it in an airtight container.
Consume two cups of the cooled ginger tea solution three times daily for a minimum period of four weeks to alleviate ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with ringworm because its antifungal properties provide a natural solution to combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. The tea's high concentration of citral, a compound found in lemongrass, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healing. Additionally, lemongrass contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with ringworm. Drinking lemongrass tea regularly can aid in reducing symptoms such as itching, redness, and scaling, allowing the body to recover from the infection more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Lemongrass tea contains antifungal compounds like citral, which help to inhibit the growth and spread of fungal infections like ringworm, reducing symptoms and promoting healing.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in lemongrass tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by ringworm, which can aid in wound recovery and reduce scarring.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, like citral and geraniol, help to alleviate inflammation and redness associated with ringworm lesions, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have anti-fungal properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, a modified underground stem, contains antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that target fungal growth and alleviate symptoms of ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh lemongrass leaves and stalks for each 8-ounce serving of tea.
Rinse the harvested lemongrass thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the rinsed lemongrass into small pieces to release its essential oils and flavor compounds.
Steep 1 tablespoon of chopped lemongrass in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a flavorful tea infusion.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids, then drink the tea immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras teas helps with ringworm because of its natural antifungal properties, which target the fungus that causes the infection. The tea's active compounds, such as safrole and limonene, have been shown to inhibit fungal growth and promote skin health. When consumed regularly, sassafras tea can help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, while also promoting a healthy immune response. This natural approach can be especially beneficial for individuals who prefer alternative treatments or experience sensitivity to conventional antifungal medications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sassafras tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and spread of the causative fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sassafras tea reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, making it an effective treatment for soothing skin irritations and promoting recovery.
Antifungal properties: Sassafras tea's antifungal properties specifically target the fungi that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton rubrum, killing them off and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in safrole, a natural antifungal compound that helps combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: Contains lignans and sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against skin irritations caused by ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried sassafras albidum root bark and one quart of boiling water.
Steep the sassafras albidum root bark in boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean container using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Combine two cups of cooled tea with one tablespoon of baking soda and one teaspoon of coconut oil.
Drink the sassafras albidum tea twice daily for seven days, then discontinue treatment if symptoms improve.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal teas are fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and toenail fungus. Certain herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, and goldenseal have antifungal properties that help soothe and combat these infections. The teas can be used topically or taken internally to promote healing and reduce symptoms like itching, burning, and discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ringworm are a natural remedy that involves steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing tea that helps alleviate the symptoms of ringworm. These teas typically contain antifungal properties, which combat the fungal infection causing the ringworm. Examples of herbal teas that help with ringworm include tea tree oil, neem, and echinacea. Drinking these teas can reduce itching and inflammation, improving the quality of life for individuals with ringworm. This natural approach promotes healing and reduces the risk of scarring.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with ringworm is a blend of tea tree oil, calendula, and goldenseal. Tea tree oil has antifungal properties that combat the fungus causing ringworm, while calendula soothes and calms the affected skin. Goldenseal, with its antibacterial and antifungal properties, helps to accelerate the healing process. Drinking this tea combination can aid in fighting ringworm from the inside out, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of recurrence.
